qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Pre-authentication client version parsing regex allows low-cost request blocking
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding is a pre-auth request-parsing regex stall in `handleParseHeaders` and `ClientSDK.fromString`; the advisory fixes `findUsersWithAuthData` in `src/Auth.js` to skip database lookups for unconfigured auth providers, so the vulnerable code and root cause differ.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: GraphQL validation suggestions disclose schema metadata to unauthenticated callers
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding is GraphQL validation-error suggestion leakage in `ParseGraphQLServer`; the advisory fixes the auth-provider lookup path in `src/Auth.js`, so this is a different component, endpoint, and root cause.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory root cause: Parse Server's `src/Auth.js` queried the user database for unconfigured auth providers before rejecting them, and the patch adds an early validator check to prevent those unindexed lookups.
evidence: Advisory commits `40eb442e02672986730007d0a1edb22c1c4bd357` and `fbac847499e57f243315c5fc7135be1d58bb8e54` modify `src/Auth.js` `findUsersWithAuthData` to return early when `getValidatorForProvider(provider)` has no validator, preventing database `find` calls for unconfigured providers.

`client_version_regex_redos` reports regex backtracking in `repo/src/ClientSDK.js` and `repo/src/middlewares.js` during header/body parsing before authentication, not the advisory's auth-provider lookup path.
`graphql_schema_suggestion_disclosure` reports Apollo validation suggestion leakage in `repo/src/GraphQL/ParseGraphQLServer.js`, not the advisory's unconfigured-provider database query behavior.
